pluto.description: The most famous dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt. Formerly the ninth planet, now classified as a dwarf planet.
funFacts: Pluto is smaller than Earth's Moon
One year on Pluto equals 248 Earth years
Pluto has an ice heart visible from New Horizons photos

ceres.description: The largest object in the asteroid belt. The only dwarf planet in the inner Solar System.
funFacts: Ceres holds 25% of the asteroid belt's mass
It may have an ocean of water beneath its surface
Discovered in 1801, originally classified as a planet

eris.description: The most massive dwarf planet in the Solar System. Named after the Greek goddess of discord.
funFacts: Eris is more massive than Pluto
One orbit of Eris takes 559 years

haumea.description: An ellipsoidal dwarf planet due to its super-fast rotation. Named after the Hawaiian goddess of fertility.
funFacts: Haumea rotates every 4 hours — the fastest in the Solar System
Its shape is like a rugby ball due to rapid rotation
It has 2 small moons: Hi'iaka and Namaka

makemake.description: A dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt. Named after the Rapa Nui creator god.
funFacts: Makemake is one of 3 dwarf planets in the Kuiper Belt
